summary: Joel briefly promotes the Joel on Software reader meetups organized through Meetup.com, which take place in 640 cities on the third Wednesday of each month. He explains the basic mechanics of how meetups require 5 confirmed members to proceed. He also gives a nod to Meetup.com founder Scott Heiferman, a fellow New Yorker.
key_points: Joel on Software reader meetups were organized globally through Meetup.com in 640 cities
Meetups required a minimum of 5 confirmed members to avoid automatic cancellation
Meetup.com was founded by Scott Heiferman, who previously founded i-traffic.com
money_quotes: Meetup.com organizes regularly scheduled Joel on Software reader meetings in 640 cities around the world on the third Wednesday of every month.
key_insight: A brief community announcement promoting in-person Joel on Software reader meetups organized through Meetup.com, notable mainly as a snapshot of early 2000s tech community building.
